title: Shutdown storage on failures
body:
Buildah is leaving storage enabled when commands fail.
Specifically we have seen this with buildah from.
This can lead to a leaked mount point on the system.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I'd like a test also. It should be easy, and shouldn't even require a new test: maybe add `mount | grep $TESTDIR` and barf if it succeeds, somewhere around here in the `buildah pull never` test:

https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/190c51e8ec7afb2eaa870fb616c4d6ca45ea3097/tests/from.bats#L281
The problem is this leak is not happening with vfs, only with overlay.  So the test never fails.
Tests green. Thank you for adding the new one. LGTM.
